# Jellyfin Web 10.11.11 Theme-Making Guide

This is a beginner-friendly and technical guide to styling Jellyfin Web 10.11.11 with Custom CSS.

It explains what each part of Jellyfin's interface is, what you can usually change with CSS, and gives examples for every selector found by this documentation project.

## Plain-English Basics

A **CSS selector** is the name you write before a CSS block. It tells the browser what part of the page you want to style.

Example:

```css
.cardBox {
  border-radius: 8px;
}
```

In that example, `.cardBox` is the selector. It targets Jellyfin card boxes, such as poster cards or media tiles.

## How To Read Each Entry

Each selector entry answers the same practical questions:

- **What it is** explains the Jellyfin screen area in normal language.
- **Selector type** explains whether the selector targets one screen, a reusable class, a built-in Jellyfin web component, an attribute, or a state like `:hover`.
- **Where it appears in source** gives the Jellyfin Web files that mention it, so advanced theme makers can inspect the original context.
- **What you can usually change** lists CSS property families that are realistic for that selector.
- **Example** gives a complete CSS block you can paste into Jellyfin's Custom CSS box and then adjust.

The examples are starting points, not rules. A selector for a page wrapper can often use background, spacing, and layout properties. A selector for a poster/card can often use radius, shadow, image sizing, and hover effects. A selector for a form control can often use border, background, focus color, height, and font styling.

## Selector Types In Plain English

- `#loginPage` starts with `#`, so it targets one specific page or unique element.
- `.cardBox` starts with `.`, so it targets a reusable class that may appear in many places.
- `emby-input` has no `.` or `#`, so it targets a custom Jellyfin web element.
- `[data-type]` targets elements that carry a specific HTML data attribute. These are useful for advanced targeting, but they can be broad.
- `.button:hover` targets a state. It applies only while the user is hovering over that element.

## What CSS Can And Cannot Do

CSS can change colors, spacing, borders, shadows, fonts, visibility, layout, hover/focus states, image fitting, and many responsive behaviors.

CSS cannot reliably add new Jellyfin features, fetch different media, change server data, rewrite page logic, or create a fully new home-screen carousel by itself. Those changes need JavaScript changes, a Jellyfin Web fork, or a plugin-level approach.

## Version Scope

- Jellyfin Web version: `10.11.11`
- Source tag: `v10.11.11`
- Source commit inspected: `35c0793ece3adbd247eab290ae1effab851f3d37`
- Stylesheet files scanned: 108
- Markup/script files scanned: 841
- Selectors documented: 2341

## How This Guide Is Organized

- The sections below match parts of the Jellyfin screen: pages, cards, lists, forms, playback, dashboard, and so on.
- Every selector has a plain-language explanation.
- Every selector has an example CSS block.
- The [Directory](DIRECTORY.md) is the same information alphabetized like a glossary.

## Complete Guide


## Pages and Full Screens

These selectors target entire screens. They are the easiest way to make one page look different from the rest of Jellyfin.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 62 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `#addPluginPage`

- **What it is:** This is the main wrapper for the add Plugin screen. Use it when you want CSS to affect that whole screen only.
- **Selector type:** an ID selector, usually one unique element on the page. Found as: ID used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/apps/dashboard/routes/plugins/plugin.tsx`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - layout styling such as display, width, max-width, height, margin, padding, gap, alignment, and overflow.
- **Example:**

```css
#addPluginPage {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.5rem;
}
```

## App Shell Layout and Spacing

These are the broad wrappers and layout helpers that shape the whole Jellyfin Web app.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 8 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `.align-items-flex-end`

- **What it is:** This selector appears in the App Shell Layout and Spacing area. The name breaks down roughly as: "align items flex end".
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/styles/flexstyles.scss`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
- **Example:**

```css
.align-items-flex-end {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.5rem;
}
```

## Home Screen Rows

}

## Cards Posters and Artwork

These control posters, backdrops, square tiles, people cards, library cards, fallback artwork, overlays, and card labels.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 50 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `.backdropImage`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/components/backdrop/backdrop.scss`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - image/icon styling such as object-fit, background-size, filter, aspect ratio, and icon size.
- **Example:**

```css
.backdropImage {
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.

  - image/icon styling such as object-fit, background-size, filter, aspect ratio, and icon size.
  - interactive states such as :hover, :focus, :active, :disabled, and selected/checked states.
- **Example:**

```css
.portraitCard {
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

## Libraries Search Filters and Sorting

These control library pages, search results, filter dialogs, sort controls, alpha pickers, genres, years, studios, artists, albums, and view settings.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 70 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `.addLibrary`

- **What it is:** This selector appears in the Libraries Search Filters and Sorting area. The name breaks down roughly as: "add Library".
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: class used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/apps/wizard/controllers/library.js`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
- **Example:**

```css
.addLibrary {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.5rem;
}
```

#### `.backdropCard-scalable`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: class used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/apps/wizard/controllers/library.js`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - layout styling such as display, width, max-width, height, margin, padding, gap, alignment, and overflow.
  - image/icon styling such as object-fit, background-size, filter, aspect ratio, and icon size.
  - interactive states such as :hover, :focus, :active, :disabled, and selected/checked states.
- **Example:**

```css
.backdropCard-scalable {
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

#### `.listItemImage`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.

}

## Forms Inputs and Settings Controls

These control all form elements: inputs, selects, labels, helper text, checkboxes, radio buttons, sliders, toggles, and settings panels.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 188 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

}

- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/elements/emby-slider/emby-slider.scss`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - interactive states such as :hover, :focus, :active, :disabled, and selected/checked states.
- **Example:**

```css
.mdl-slider {
  background: #1f1f1f;
  color: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #555;
  border-radius: 6px;
}
```

## Buttons Icons and Actions

These control clickable actions: play buttons, submit buttons, icon buttons, cancel/delete buttons, tabs, active states, and Material Icons glyphs.

## Dialogs Menus Toasts and Popups

These control popups, modals, action sheets, menus, context menus, prompts, confirmations, overlays, and toast messages.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 128 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `.actionSheet`

- **What it is:** This is part of a popup-style interface: dialogs, action sheets, menus, prompts, context menus, or toast notifications.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/components/actionSheet/actionSheet.scss`, `src/themes/appletv/theme.scss`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
- **Example:**

```css
.actionSheet {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-radius: 10px;
}
```

#### `.actionSheetContent`

- **What it is:** This is part of a popup-style interface: dialogs, action sheets, menus, prompts, context menus, or toast notifications.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class, class used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/components/actionSheet/actionSheet.scss`, `src/components/actionSheet/actionSheet.ts`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - layout styling such as display, width, max-width, height, margin, padding, gap, alignment, and overflow.
- **Example:**

```css
.actionSheetContent {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-radius: 10px;
}
```

#### `.actionSheetItemAsideText`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: CSS stylesheet class, class used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/components/actionSheet/actionSheet.scss`, `src/components/actionSheet/actionSheet.ts`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - text styling such as font-size, font-weight, line-height, text-transform, and text-shadow.
- **Example:**

```css
.actionSheetItemAsideText {
  color: #f5f5f5;
  font-weight: 700;
  text-shadow: 0 1px 3px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.45);
}
```

#### `.actionsheetListItemBody`

- **Example:**

```css
.actionsheetListItemBody {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border: 1px solid #444;
  border-radius: 10px;
}
```

#### `.headerButtonLeft`

- **What it is:** This is related to a clickable button, icon button, or link action. It may control the button itself, an icon inside it, or a specific action button.

- **Example:**

```css

  color: #ffffff;
  background: #005f8f;
  border-radius: 8px;
}

.headerButtonLeft:hover {
  background: #008bd2;
}
```

## Indicators Progress and Status

These control badges and state markers: watched indicators, counts, progress bars, loading rings, recording icons, sync indicators, and task progress.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 65 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

## Playback Now Playing and OSD

These control video/audio playback UI: OSD controls, Now Playing bar, queue, subtitles, chapters, volume, skip buttons, and Up Next.

#### `.cardColumn`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.
- **Selector type:** a class selector, usually reusable on one or more elements. Found as: class used in Jellyfin markup.

  - image/icon styling such as object-fit, background-size, filter, aspect ratio, and icon size.
  - interactive states such as :hover, :focus, :active, :disabled, and selected/checked states.
- **Example:**

```css
.cardColumn {
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

## Live TV Guide and Recording

## Lyrics Subtitles and Text Tracks

These control lyrics pages, subtitle editors, subtitle sync, subtitle preview windows, and playback subtitle overlay styling.

- **Example:**

  overflow: hidden;
}
```

- **What it is:** This is related to a clickable button, icon button, or link action. It may control the button itself, an icon inside it, or a specific action button.

}

  background: #008bd2;
}
```

#### `#imageOutput`

- **What it is:** This is part of Jellyfin's card/artwork system. It can affect posters, backdrops, people cards, library tiles, card text, overlays, or fallback artwork.
- **Selector type:** an ID selector, usually one unique element on the page. Found as: ID used in Jellyfin markup.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/components/imageUploader/imageUploader.template.html`.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
  - image/icon styling such as object-fit, background-size, filter, aspect ratio, and icon size.
- **Example:**

```css
#imageOutput {
  border-radius: 8px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.35);
  overflow: hidden;
}
```

## Screensavers Slideshows and Swiper

These control screensavers, slideshows, Swiper slides, image text, pagination, and navigation arrows.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 8 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

- **What it is:** This selector appears in the Screensavers Slideshows and Swiper area. The name breaks down roughly as: "screensaver no Scroll".

  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.

## Specialty Players and Built-in Plugins

These control built-in specialty players and plugin UI: books, PDFs, comics, photos, YouTube, Chromecast, SyncPlay, and related controls.

#### `emby-button`

- **What it is:** This is related to a clickable button, icon button, or link action. It may control the button itself, an icon inside it, or a specific action button.
- **Selector type:** a Jellyfin custom element/control. Found as: Jellyfin custom element, custom Jellyfin element in CSS.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/apps/dashboard/controllers/livetvtuner.html`, `src/apps/dashboard/controllers/networking.html`, `src/apps/wizard/controllers/finish/index.html`, `src/apps/wizard/controllers/library.html`, `src/apps/wizard/controllers/remote/index.html`, plus 58 more.

  - interactive states such as :hover, :focus, :active, :disabled, and selected/checked states.
- **Example:**

```css
emby-button {
  color: #ffffff;
  background: #005f8f;
  border-radius: 8px;
}

emby-button:hover {
  background: #008bd2;
}
```

## Data Attribute Selectors

These selectors target HTML attributes like data-type and data-action. They are useful for styling elements based on what Jellyfin says they are or do.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 98 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `[data-action]`

- **What it is:** This targets elements that Jellyfin marks with the data-action attribute. Use it to style elements only when that data marker is present.
- **Selector type:** an attribute selector, used when Jellyfin marks an element with extra data. Found as: data attribute selector.
- **Where it appears in source:** `src/apps/experimental/features/details/components/buttons/PlayOrResumeButton.tsx`, `src/components/cardbuilder/Card/CardHoverMenu.tsx`, `src/components/cardbuilder/cardBuilder.js`, `src/components/cardbuilder/chaptercardbuilder.js`, `src/components/common/InfoIconButton.tsx`, plus 14 more.
- **What you can usually change:**
  - visual styling such as color, background, border, shadow, opacity, and border-radius.
- **Example:**

```css
[data-action] .cardBox {
  background: #202020;
  color: #ffffff;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 0.5rem;
}
```

## Utilities and Miscellaneous

These are helper classes and leftover source items that do not fit cleanly elsewhere. Some are useful; others are internal or situational.

<details open>
<summary><strong>All 20 selectors in this section</strong></summary>

#### `.clear_all`
